notes: Sakura Hotel Group — NFPA 72 + ISO 7240 fire safety audit, Nihonbashi flagship property

ledger_entries.note: Peak summer occupancy + emergency generator testing cycle — HVAC at full load
December value estimated from booking forecast and prior year pattern
Estimated from weather forecast and occupancy projection

review_items.title: NFPA 72 §14.4: 18 smoke detectors past replacement date
review_items.description: 18 ionization smoke detectors in floors B1-3 installed 2014, exceeding 10-year replacement requirement per NFPA 72-2022 §14.4.5.3.

review_items.title: Fire door inspection: 8 doors with failed self-closing mechanisms
review_items.description: Fire door inspection log shows 8 of 156 rated fire doors (5.1%) fail to self-close completely. Japanese Building Standards Law Art.27 requires 100% compliance.

review_items.title: Emergency lighting: 12 units below minimum lux level
review_items.description: Annual test shows 12 of 245 emergency lighting units producing <1 lux at floor level (minimum: 1 lux per ISO 7240-1). Battery degradation suspected.

review_items.title: Kitchen hood suppression system: nozzle alignment off-spec
review_items.description: Semi-annual test of Ansul R-102 system in main kitchen shows 2 of 8 nozzles misaligned after ductwork modification. NFPA 96 §12.1 violation.

review_items.title: Evacuation time exceeds target: Floor 12-15 at 6.2 min vs 4 min target
review_items.description: Annual drill showed upper floors (12-15) evacuation time 6.2 minutes, exceeding 4-minute design target. Stairwell B congestion identified.

review_items.title: December energy values estimated
review_items.description: Both electricity and gas December values projected. Confidence 68-70%. Final bills needed for report.

review_items.title: June-August electricity spike correlates with HVAC overload
review_items.description: Summer peak 168,000-175,000 kWh vs. shoulder season 128,000-140,000 kWh. HVAC system age (2008) may indicate efficiency degradation.
review_items.reviewer_note: Expected seasonal pattern for Tokyo hotel. HVAC efficiency assessment scheduled for Q1 2026.

audit_log.detail: Case CASE-004 created — fire safety audit
Fire alarm as-built drawings and sprinkler reports uploaded
Evacuation plan and fire door logs uploaded
Extraction complete — 664 data points, fire safety systems indexed
Summer HVAC pattern confirmed as seasonal
NFPA 72 / ISO 7240 fire safety report draft generated

measures.title: Smoke Detector Replacement — Floors B1-3 (18 units)
measures.description: Replace 18 aging ionization smoke detectors with addressable multi-sensor detectors (combined optical + heat) per NFPA 72-2022 §14.4.5.3 10-year replacement requirement.

evidence.measurement: Detector installation dates from maintenance database: all 18 units installed 2014-03, age = 11 years (exceeds 10-year limit)
evidence.nameplate: Replacing: 18x Hochiki SLR-835B ionization. Installing: 18x Hochiki ALN-EN multi-sensor, addressable, EN 54-7/54-5
evidence.method: Mandatory compliance — no financial payback calculation applicable. Non-compliance penalty: license suspension risk per Japanese Fire Service Act Art.17
evidence.price_basis: Hochiki ALN-EN: ¥65,000/unit installed (incl. panel programming). 18 units = ¥1,170,000 (€7,200). Additional commissioning + testing: ¥1,170,000 (€7,200)
evidence.confidence_note: Mandatory replacement — installation dates verified from maintenance records and Hochiki serial number database

measures.title: Fire Door Self-Closer Repair and Upgrade (8 doors)
measures.description: Replace failed door closers on 8 fire-rated doors and install electromagnetic hold-open devices connected to the fire alarm panel for 12 high-traffic corridor doors.

evidence.measurement: Fire door inspection log: 8 doors fail self-close test (EN 1154 force measurement: 4 doors <8N, 4 doors with broken arm linkage)
evidence.nameplate: 8x Dorma TS 93 closers (replacing GEZE TS 2000 NV from 2010). 12x Dorma EM hold-open devices + interface modules to Hochiki FIREnet panel
evidence.method: Cost avoidance: fire insurance premium reduction ¥1,300,000/year (confirmed by Tokio Marine underwriter) for full door compliance
evidence.price_basis: Dorma TS 93: ¥42,000/door installed. EM hold-open: ¥68,000/door installed. Total ¥1,320,000 = €12,800
evidence.confidence_note: Door inventory 100% inspected. Insurance premium reduction confirmed in writing by underwriter.

measures.title: Emergency Lighting Battery Replacement Program
measures.description: Replace NiCd batteries in 12 failed emergency lighting units and transition entire property (245 units) to LED emergency luminaires with LiFePO4 batteries over 3-year cycle.

evidence.measurement: Annual emergency lighting test (EN 50172): 12 units <1 lux at floor level (min 1 lux required). Battery voltage test: 12 units below 85% rated capacity
evidence.nameplate: Current: 245x fluorescent emergency luminaires with NiCd 3.6V 4Ah packs. Phase 1 (12 units): Toshiba LED-22012 with LiFePO4 3.2V 6Ah
evidence.method: LED replacement saves 6W/unit × 245 units × 8,760h standby trickle charge = 8,200 kWh/year. At ¥30/kWh = ¥246,000/year (€2,460)
evidence.price_basis: TEPCO commercial rate: ¥30/kWh (2025). Toshiba LED-22012: ¥55,000/unit. 3-year program: Year 1 ¥660,000 + Year 2 ¥2,200,000 + Year 3 ¥1,640,000
evidence.confidence_note: 12 failed units verified by lux measurement. 3-year rollout cost and savings based on manufacturer lifecycle data

measures.title: Stairwell B Evacuation Flow Improvement
measures.description: Widen Stairwell B landing at floors 12-13 (structural modification), add photoluminescent wayfinding strips per ISO 16069, and install voice evacuation speakers replacing bell alarms on upper floors.

evidence.measurement: Evacuation drill data: Floor 12-15 clearance time 6.2 min (target: 4 min). Video analysis shows bottleneck at Stairwell B floor 12-13 landing (1.1m width vs. 1.4m Building Standards Law minimum for >200 occupants)
evidence.nameplate: Structural: landing widening from 1.1m to 1.5m (reinforced concrete modification). Voice evacuation: 24x TOA F-2352C speakers + RM-300MF amplifier rack
evidence.method: Engineering estimate: widening reduces peak flow density from 1.8 p/m/s to 1.2 p/m/s, projected clearance time reduction to 3.8 min (Pathfinder simulation model)
evidence.price_basis: Structural modification: ¥12,000,000. Voice evacuation system: ¥7,000,000. Design + permitting: ¥350,000. Total: ¥19,350,000 (€185,000)
evidence.confidence_note: Structural feasibility confirmed by Taisei Corp preliminary assessment. Evacuation time improvement based on Pathfinder simulation — actual improvement depends on occupant behavior
